rationale: Both hit the latent gap (X very concretely, by naming five existing tests that call sleep(level=2)+wake_up() and would now break — closely mirroring the real #4905 CI break; Y frames it as the default level=2 'bricking' the common path). X's top 'blocker' claim (stale `_is_sleeping` attribute assignment 'will raise AttributeError') is almost certainly wrong for a normal Python object without __slots__, hurting its precision, whereas Y's findings (default-level footgun, tag-set vs None semantics, dead getattr guards, pre-existing desync noted as low-severity) are all well-grounded and calibrated, and Y is the only one to directly engage the ground-truth reviewer's 'separate place for the enum' comment. Actionability is comparably strong on both sides with concrete file/line fixes.
